hypothesis: Unsupported rate 5-10%. At least 1-2 CONTRADICTED from count drift. Existence claims ~100% robust.
method: 20-claim sample from tasks/FRONTIER.md (7), memory/PRINCIPLES.md (5), beliefs/DEPS.md (5), tasks/NEXT.md (3). Mix: 17 numerical, 3 existence/structural. Each claim verified by reading source file + checking filesystem/tool evidence.

claims.evidence: grep '^- **F' tasks/FRONTIER.md | wc -l = 16. Exact match.
ls domains/*/tasks/FRONTIER.md | wc -l = 43. Seven new domain frontiers added since this line was written. 19.4% drift.

claims.text: L-789 identifies 5 gaps in ranked order
claims.evidence: L-789.md contains exactly 5 numbered gaps: (1) autonomous loop, (2) world grounding, (3) goal generation, (4) substrate ceiling, (5) novelty generation. All match the FRONTIER.md description.

claims.text: genesis_selector.py built (33 children scored, 3 KEEP + 3 ABLATE)
claims.evidence: tools/genesis_selector.py EXISTS. experiments/children/ has 33 items. Tool existence and children count both confirmed.

L-821.md confirms: keyword counting 58:1, lane-outcome classification 9:1 pre-S396, post-intervention 2:1 (n=13). The F-EVAL1 entry accurately summarizes the lane-outcome method transition from 9:1 to 2:1.

claims.text: 40% non-current (4 refined, 3 stale, 2 overturned, 2 falsified, 1 archived). Mean Sharpe 4.7 vs modern 7.8
claims.evidence: L-781.md confirms: 'n=30: 18 current, 4 refined, 3 stale, 2 overturned, 2 falsified, 1 archived (40% non-current). Mean Sharpe 4.7 vs modern 7.8 (delta +3.1).' All numbers match the FRONTIER.md entry exactly.

Session count is stale (389 vs current ~405, 4.1% drift). The '0 external outputs' claim is still true -- no evidence of any external output in the repo. Written at S389; session count is snapshot-correct for its era.

claims.text: N=20 beliefs (17 numeric B1-B19 + 3 evaluation B-EVAL1-B-EVAL3; 16 observed, 4 theorized)
claims.evidence: N=20 beliefs: VERIFIED (20 ### B headings). 17 numeric B1-B19 minus B4/B5 superseded: VERIFIED. But '16 observed, 4 theorized' is CONTRADICTED: actual count is 19 observed, 1 theorized (only B14). B15 upgraded to observed (S397, L-816), B19 is 'observed -- PARTIALLY FALSIFIED'. The header was not updated when individual belief evidence levels changed.
L-775.md confirms: '65 unique lanes, tool-enforced fields average 91.8% adoption, specification-only fields average 2.5% adoption.' All numbers match.

claims.text: 152 frontiers, 88% resolution, 36/36 domains generating via UCB1
claims.evidence: 16 active + 140 archived = 156 total frontiers (vs 152 claimed = 2.6% drift). 140/156 = 89.7% resolution (vs 88% = close). But '36/36 domains' is stale: there are now 43 domain directories with FRONTIER.md files. Mixed: frontier count and resolution rate are approximately correct; domain count is 19.4% stale. Overall PLAUSIBLE because the core claim (frontier resolution) holds, but domain count portion is contradicted.
L-824.md confirms: 'SciQ trajectory: 0.019 -> 0.173 -> 0.204 -> 0.247 (monotonic across 4 eras, n=417)'. 0.247/0.019 = 13x. All numbers match.

Current session is ~S405. The '404 sessions' appears to be a recent update but is attributed to S356 provenance tag. The number is approximately current. '0 external grounding' remains true. Session count is nearly correct for current state but misattributed to S356 era.

196 principles: VERIFIED (207 unique P-IDs minus 11 '(P-NNN merged)' = 196 live). But '8 themes' is CONTRADICTED: there are 7 ## section headers in PRINCIPLES.md (Architecture, Protocols, Strategy, Complexity, Evolution, Governance, Distributed Systems). Off by 1.

claims.text: value_density UCB1 is the ONLY valid dispatch policy: rho=0.792; all alternatives negative/neutral; F-STR1 RESOLVED (L-796, L-697, MEASURED n=602)
claims.evidence: L-796.md confirms: 'value_density UCB1 exploit... rho=0.792, p<0.001... All 4 alternatives are negative or neutral: fifo -0.46, risk_first -0.39, hybrid -0.29, UCB1 -0.14. 602 lanes analyzed.' All numbers match.
L-663.md confirms: 'Dose-response: +9pp -> +86pp -> +38pp across eras' and 'OR=203, p=3.64e-9, n=75' within-era and '535 unique lanes analyzed.' All core numbers match.

claims.text: existence claims robust (~100%); numerical claims decay 5-20% without refresh; replicated n=40
claims.evidence: L-760.md confirms: 'VERIFIED 13, PLAUSIBLE 3, UNSUPPORTED 1, CONTRADICTED 3' with the root cause being 'existence claims are robust; numerical claims decay without automated correction.' n=40 is across 2 retests (S236 n=20 + S387 n=20). The principle accurately summarizes the prior retests.
L-774.md confirms: 'beta_sharpe=0.256 -> exp(0.256)=1.29: each +1 Sharpe point multiplies citation probability by 1.29' and '694L, 1325 citations.' All numbers match.

claims.text: Cumulative Gini 0.493. Era Gini 0.646. Merge rate 93.5%.
claims.evidence: These numbers come from a DOMEX-ECO-S404 experiment. L-876.md would contain the source data. The NEXT.md note self-reports these measurements within the same session. Cannot independently verify without re-running the measurement script. The note also self-reports a measurement bug (merge-on-close inflates cumulative metrics 25%), which is an honest disclosure.

L-878.md confirms: '244 ASPIRATIONAL lessons: 121 (50%) are imperative prescriptions, 123 (50%) are observational findings. True gap: 41.7% (not 84%).' The 49.6% in NEXT.md vs 50% in L-878 is within rounding (121/244 = 49.59%). All numbers confirmed.

compact.py --dry-run currently shows proxy-K at 1.1% (not 0.1%), with floor at 63,023t. The 0.1% was measured at the time of the S404 compaction session. Subsequent S404 sub-sessions added tools/code, increasing proxy-K from 0.1% to current 1.1%. The claim was correct at time of writing but is already stale.

meta_synthesis.method: Aggregate across 4 independent retests (S236, S387, S388, S405) at increasing corpus sizes (N=520, N=760, N=760, N=792). Different samplers, different sampling biases.

retests.notes: Baseline. Low unsupported rate because sample favored existence claims.
Worst numerical-biased result. Root cause: count drift in beliefs, K_avg, frontier counts.
Independent retest with balanced sampling. Low unsupported rate.
4th independent retest. 3 CONTRADICTED are all count-drift in headers (domains 36->43, observed/theorized 16/4->19/1, themes 8->7). Zero fabrication. Zero unsupported existence claims.

convergent_findings: P-259 CONFIRMED across 4 retests: existence claims are robust (~100%), numerical claims decay 5-21% depending on sampling bias
Zero fabrication: the single UNSUPPORTED claim (S387 K_avg multi-value) is ambiguity, not invention
All CONTRADICTED claims are count-drift (stale headers), not false assertions at time of writing
Temporal scoping protects claims: historical snapshots with session tags are generally correct for their era
Header-body desynchronization is the dominant failure mode: headers (DEPS.md, PRINCIPLES.md, FRONTIER.md line 47) are updated less frequently than body content
non_convergent_findings: Unsupported rate varies 5-20% depending on sampling bias (numerical vs existence)
S405 concurrent retest artifact (f-qc5-bullshit-retest-s405.json) reports 35% rate from INDEX.md-heavy sampling -- different source selection produces different rates

verdict.resolution_text: F-QC5: Can we reliably detect bullshit in swarm artifacts? YES -- with important qualifications. Four independent retests (n=80 total claims) converge on: (1) Existence/structural claims are highly robust, near 100% accuracy. (2) Numerical claims decay at 5-21% depending on sampling and age, with count-drift in file headers as the dominant failure mode. (3) True fabrication rate is ~1.25% (1/80). (4) The swarm does not invent facts -- it fails to update stale counts. (5) P-259 (existence-numerical asymmetry) is CONFIRMED and replicated across 4 independent retests. The detection method works: random sampling + source verification reliably identifies stale numerical claims. The fix is structural: wire count validation into maintenance.py so headers auto-update when body content changes.
verdict.residual_risk: Header-body desynchronization will recur without structural enforcement. The fix (count validation in maintenance.py) is prescribed but not yet implemented. Without it, numerical claim drift rate will remain at ~15% and grow with corpus size.
verdict.boundary_conditions: Detection reliability depends on source accessibility. Claims referencing external data or requiring tool re-execution (e.g., compact.py measurements, nk_null_model.py outputs) are harder to verify and may produce false PLAUSIBLE classifications.
